text.title: AWS Serverless API (Lambda + API Gateway + DynamoDB + EventBridge) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules pinning the serverless API non-negotiables.

rule.statement: Lambda functions MUST use architectures=['arm64'], tracing_mode='Active', runtime ≥python3.12 or ≥nodejs22.x.
rule.rationale: arm64 is ~20% cheaper and faster than x86 for Python/Node. Active X-Ray is required for distributed tracing across Lambda + API GW + DDB.

rule.statement: API Gateway MUST be HTTP API (protocol_type='HTTP'). REST API (v1) is forbidden for new serverless projects.
rule.rationale: HTTP API is ~70% cheaper + 60% faster than REST API for the common case. REST API only required for VPC Link / API key / WAF integration.

rule.statement: CORS allow_origins MUST be an explicit list. '*' is forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Wildcard CORS exposes the API to any origin; combined with credentialed requests it's a CSRF / data-exfiltration vector.
Unthrottled API GW lets a burst of requests drive up Lambda concurrent executions, costing money + tripping account-level limits.

rule.statement: Production APIs MUST use a custom domain with ACM cert. The default `*.execute-api.region.amazonaws.com` URL is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Default URL exposes account ID + region in the hostname; not stable across redeploys; cannot be CDN-fronted.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
